title: Is The Estee Lauder (EL) Modestly Undervalued? A Comprehensive Analysis
text: On July 28, 2023, The Estee Lauder Companies Inc ( EL , Financial) recorded a daily gain of 4.12% and reported an Earnings Per Share (EPS) of $3.01. But is the stock modestly undervalued?

text: The Estee Lauder Companies Inc ( EL , Financial), a leading player in the Consumer Packaged Goods industry, is currently trading at $180.11 with a market cap of $64.37 billion. Despite a 7.03% loss over the past four weeks, the stock has seen a gain of 3.84% today.

title: 7 Growth Stocks to Buy Before They Mint Millionaires

title: Estee Lauder was victim of cybersecurity hack that has disrupted part of its business operations
text: Estee Lauder Cos. EL said Wednesday it has been hacked by an unauthorized third party and has taken down some of its systems as it investigates what data has been accessed.

title: Estee Lauder hit by hack, some business operations affected
text: Cosmetics maker Estee Lauder on Tuesday said a hacker had obtained some data from its systems, with the cyber incident causing, and expected to further cause, disruption to parts of the company's business operations.

title: The Estée Lauder Companies Inc. Provides Information on Cybersecurity Incident
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Estée Lauder Companies Inc. (NYSE: EL) has identified a cybersecurity incident, which involves an unauthorized third party that has gained access to some of the Company's systems. After becoming aware of the incident, the Company proactively took down some of its systems and promptly began an investigation with the assistance of leading third-party cybersecurity experts. The Company is also coordinating with law enforcement. Based on the current status of the inve.

title: Estee Lauder Stock: Buy, Sell, or Hold?
text: Estee Lauder's performance is suffering as economic trends play against it, but the long-term outlook looks bright. The company is still profitable, and it pays a growing dividend.

title: COTY or EL: Which Is the Better Value Stock Right Now?
text: Investors interested in stocks from the Cosmetics sector have probably already heard of Coty (COTY) and Estee Lauder (EL). But which of these two stocks is more attractive to value investors?

title: Why Estée Lauder Stock Dropped 21% in the First Half of 2023
text: Estée Lauder owns a large suite of upscale cosmetics brands. Sales and income are declining year over year, and management lowered full-year guidance.

title: Estee Lauder Stock: Well-Positioned For Long-Term Growth
text: Estee Lauder's stock is down by 22% year to date. Estee Lauder's strong brand presence helped them dominate the beauty market. The company's plans to close underperforming stores will minimize costs and boost e-commerce sales.

title: The Estée Lauder Companies Shares New Preclinical and Clinical Findings at World Congress of Dermatology Spanning Emerging Research Areas that Support Skin Health and Longevity
text: Findings indicate sirtuins, or longevity proteins, are instrumental in regulating the mechanobiology of the skin and promoting youthful skin properties and function First-of-its-kind study confirms impact of regular use of moisturizer to mitigate skin aging Topical treatment proven as viable alternative to dermatological procedure NEW YORK , July 6, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- The Estée Lauder Companies Inc. (ELC) (NYSE: EL) today announced that the company and several of its brands presented a range of novel preclinical and clinical findings at the 25th World Congress of Dermatology, held this week in Singapore. The poster and satellite symposium presentations reflect a strong portfolio of prestige beauty and skin care brands effectively translating biological learnings and progressive research efforts into meaningful innovation, including better methods to care for and protect skin.

title: Estee Lauder (EL) Gains From Emerging Market Presence Amid Risks
text: Estee Lauder (EL) is poised to benefit from its online business, investments in new technology and digital experiences, as well as its strength in emerging markets. However, high operating expenses remain a concern.
